race.name: Granfondo Charlevoix
race.tagline: Rolling Charlevoix backroads, no podium pressure.

vitals.date_specific: 2026: May 31 — two distances offered, 61 km and 87 km

climate.description: Late May in the Charlevoix highlands: cool mornings, unpredictable rain, still-crisp Laurentian air off the St. Lawrence.
challenges: Rain risk — the 2026 edition started in rain

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 53. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 51. FLAG (wave 10, not applied): sol review notes road_surface=1 (implies pristine tarmac) sits awkwardly against wave-10 research finding of frequently rough/granular pavement on the backroads sections, and field_depth=2 (implies some competitive rankings) against the event being explicitly non-competitive/finish-time-only. Numbers left unchanged pending human review.

course_description.character: A timed but non-competitive road cyclosportive through the rolling Charlevoix backcountry outside Baie-Saint-Paul — riders get a finish time, not a ranked result. Vélo Charlevoix (the regional club, formerly Club Cycliste de Charlevoix) runs two distances, 61 km and 87 km, both starting from Centre Éducatif Saint-Aubin. The 2026 edition borrowed sections of the Grand Prix Cycliste de Charlevoix's elite road-race course, covered at cyclosportive pace rather than race pace. Neither the organizer nor Quebec's cycling federation (FQSC) publishes an elevation-gain figure for either route — treat any number you see elsewhere for this race as invented, not verified.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Saint-Irénée sector — a short, steep climb regional cycling coverage describes as brutal — paired with the exposed, frequently windy Rang Saint-Laurent.
suffering_zones: Saint-Irénée ascent: short and steep, singled out in regional press as the hardest pitch on course.
Rang Saint-Laurent: rolling and consistently windy, over pavement local reporting describes as frequently rough or granular rather than smooth tarmac.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Base in Baie-Saint-Paul, the start/finish town; no organizer-recommended lodging list found.

history.origin_story: Vélo Charlevoix's cycling program traces to a first Baie-Saint-Paul Quebec Cup in 1998 and the December 1999 incorporation of Club Cycliste de Charlevoix, which rebranded as Vélo Charlevoix in 2022. Quebec's federation (FQSC) calls 2026 the '28th edition,' but that number tracks the broader Grand Rendez-Vous Cycliste de Charlevoix program — it isn't proof the mass-participation Granfondo itself has run continuously since 1999, so no founding year is asserted here.

biased_opinion.summary: Granfondo Charlevoix is exactly what it says on the tin: a timed, non-competitive ride through Quebec's Charlevoix highlands, run by the same club (Vélo Charlevoix) that puts on the elite Grand Prix Cycliste de Charlevoix the same weekend. You get two honest distances (61 km and 87 km), a finish time, and rolling, often rough Charlevoix backroads — not a podium, not a marquee international field. The organizer doesn't publish an elevation-gain figure for either route, which is a real transparency gap for a database that scores climbing; don't trust any figure you see elsewhere for this race.
strengths: Real, currently active event with a verified 2026 running (May 31) and open online registration
Two honest distance options (61 km / 87 km) for different fitness levels
Run by an established regional club with cycling roots dating to the late 1990s
2026 routes borrowed sections of the Grand Prix Cycliste de Charlevoix's elite race course
weaknesses: No published elevation-gain figure for either route — a real transparency gap
Regional reporting describes frequently rough or granular pavement on the backroads sections
Explicitly non-competitive: you get a finish time, not a ranked result
Thin public record beyond the organizer's own site and brief local press — no independent field-size or course-quality data
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Fine as a scenic, low-pressure Charlevoix ride if you're already in the region or building a Quebec cyclosportive calendar. Not worth traveling internationally for.

final_verdict.one_liner: A scenic, accessible cyclosportive through Quebec's Charlevoix region offering personal challenge without competitive pressure in a UNESCO biosphere reserve setting.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Race it if you live in or near Quebec and want a scenic, well-organized regional cyclosportive without much travel hassle. Skip it if you need a competitive field, verified climbing stats, or an event with an established international reputation.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a better-documented Quebec option with real elevation data, look at Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant. If you want the same Charlevoix roads with actual competitive stakes, the Grand Prix Cycliste de Charlevoix (same organizer, same weekend) is the elite race — but it's a licensed competition, not an open cyclosportive.

citations.label: Vélo Charlevoix — Official Granfondo Event Page

eligibility.notes: 2026 edition confirmed run May 31, 2026 from Centre Éducatif Saint-Aubin, Baie-Saint-Paul (61 km / 87 km), with live 2026 registration via Endorphine. Verified 2026-07-24 via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 10.

catalog_flags.status_note: IDENTITY CORRECTION (2026-07-24): all four previously attached YouTube videos were confirmed wrong-event and purged. 'Critérium GP Charlevoix 2018' and the 'Canada Cup #1' interview footage document the separate Grand Prix Cycliste de Charlevoix (an elite multi-stage competition run by the same club the same weekend) and a mountain-bike Canada Cup round, not this cyclosportive — the rider_intel extracted from that Canada Cup interview (hardtail gear notes, pacing tips) was also removed since it describes a different discipline and event entirely. The other two videos ('Vélo Charlevoix août 2016' and a 2017 'Grand Rendez-Vous Cyclistes' TV segment) could not be confirmed as depicting this specific event either, so the whole youtube_data block was cleared rather than left half-verified.
